description: Failure envelope: at least one reproducible, cause-labelled failure regime

cards.l4_source: Path B numerical Richardson extraction (interaction-picture transformed)

summary: D1 v0.1.2 identifies a convergence_failure regime in the sigma_x thermal fixture under the parity-aware r_4(alpha**2) = alpha**2 * <||L_4^dis||>_t / <||L_2^dis||>_t metric. All 20 frozen coupling_strength sweep points (0.05 to 1.0, log-uniform) classified as convergence_failure with stability across all four reproducibility perturbations (upper_cutoff_factor in {20, 40} now operational via the runner threading omega_max_factor into the finite-env builder, and omega_c in {9, 11} via direct model-spec mutation). Maximum baseline r_4 ~ 47.42; minimum perturbed coefficient ratio ~ 41.47 at omega_c = 11.0. v0.1.2 supersedes v0.1.1 after reviewer-side analysis surfaced two HIGH-severity defects in the v0.1.1 Path B extraction (picture/Lambda_0^-1 similarity error; trivial PASS predicate for upper_cutoff_factor perturbations) and a MEDIUM audit-completeness gap (per-alpha r_4 not persisted). v0.1.2 consumes the three repair commits (d67b453: picture transform; a908cd6: omega_max_factor threaded; 5441467: audit-complete result JSON).
limitations: The verdict uses benchmark-side Path B numerical L_4 extraction and carries the documented finite-env extraction floor (~few * 1e-2 at the default truncation per the 2026-05-06 sigma_z thermal zero-oracle pilot); analytic Path A (Companion Sec. IV) remains preferred for machine-precision evaluation and pending.
alpha_crit is not bracketed inside the frozen sweep range because the first swept point (0.05) already fails; the boundary lies below coupling_strength = 0.05 under this Path B run.
This PASS authorises a cause-labelled failure-envelope report for the sigma_x thermal fixture under the registered metric, not convergence reliability in arbitrary strong-coupling regimes.
Not analytic n=4 completion: cbg.tcl_recursion.L_n_thermal_at_time(n=4) remains deferred behind the structured Path A/B/C wall.

v011_supersedure_record.note: The v0.5.0 git tag is left as immutable history of the v0.1.1 commit; the live DG-4 verdict is v0.1.2.
